1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */

41	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily

99	/**
100	 * Cap source-exercise block count to match the structural validator's
101	 * `TOO_MANY_SECTIONS` ceiling (5). Pure: returns a NEW exercise object with
102	 * a trimmed `content.blocks` array when needed, otherwise the original.
103	 *
104	 * Picks 5 representative blocks from the source via `selectSectionsScaled` —
105	 * the same scaling-random bucketing the lesson-level selector uses. Preserves
106	 * source order, so the variation reads like a natural shorter version of the
107	 * original rather than a random shuffle.
108	 *
109	 * Rationale: without this, a 10-block source always failed validation
110	 * (TOO_MANY_SECTIONS is a blocking failure → exercise dropped from output).
111	 * Admins doing deep variations on real lessons would silently lose any
112	 * exercise that exceeded the block cap.
113	 */
114	export function trimSourceBlocksIfNeeded(exercise: ExerciseDoc): ExerciseDoc {
115	  const blocks = exercise.content?.blocks
116	  if (!Array.isArray(blocks) || blocks.length <= 5) return exercise
117	  const picked = selectSectionsForVariation(blocks, 5)
118	  logger.info(
119	    { exerciseId: exercise.id, originalCount: blocks.length, trimmedTo: picked.length },
120	    'Source exercise has more than 5 blocks — trimming via variation-aware selector',
121	  )
122	  return {
123	    ...exercise,
124	    content: { ...exercise.content, blocks: picked },
125	  }
126	}

128	/** Suggested action for a failure, based on failure code. */
129	function suggestAction(code: string): 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep' {
130	  switch (code) {
131	    case 'MISSING_QUESTION':
132	    case 'MISSING_HINT':
133	    case 'MISSING_SOLUTION':
134	    case 'MISSING_FULL_SOLUTION':
135	    case 'MISSING_CORRECT_OPTION':
136	    case 'MISSING_WRONG_OPTIONS':
137	    case 'INVALID_SVG':
138	    case 'PNG_FORBIDDEN':
139	    case 'TOO_MANY_SECTIONS':
140	    case 'INVALID_GEOMETRY_SPEC':
141	    case 'INVALID_AXIS_SPEC':
142	    case 'INVALID_GUIDED_EXPLANATION':
143	      return 'regenerate'
144	    case 'SEMANTIC_MISMATCH':
145	      return 'regenerate'
146	    case 'GENERATION_FAILED':
147	      return 'skip'
148	    default:
149	      return 'skip'
150	  }
151	}

153	// Compile-time guard: this whole module assumes CONCURRENCY_LIMIT === 1.
154	// appendEntry below uses a non-atomic read-then-update on the failures/warnings
155	// arrays — at concurrency >1 two parallel appends would race and lose entries.
156	// If you raise CONCURRENCY_LIMIT, replace appendEntry with a Mongo $push update
157	// before deleting this assert.
158	type _AssertConcurrencyOne = typeof CONCURRENCY_LIMIT extends 1 ? true : never
159	const _concurrencyAssert: _AssertConcurrencyOne = true

162	/**
163	 * Append a single failure/warning entry to the LessonDuplications record.
164	 *
165	 * The bucket name is chosen by the caller:
166	 *  - 'failures' = blocking; the exercise was dropped from the output lesson.
167	 *  - 'warnings' = non-blocking; the exercise was kept with TODO placeholders
168	 *    for the missing field. Admin polishes via the review screen.
169	 */
170	async function appendEntry(
171	  bucket: 'failures' | 'warnings',
172	  payload: Payload,
173	  duplicationId: string,
174	  exerciseRef: string,
175	  sectionIndex: number,
176	  code: string,
177	  message: string,
178	): Promise<void> {
179	  const action = suggestAction(code)
180	  try {
181	    const current = await payload.findByID({
182	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
183	      id: duplicationId,
184	      depth: 0,
185	      overrideAccess: true,
186	    })
187	    await payload.update({
188	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
189	      id: duplicationId,
190	      data: {
191	        [bucket]: [
192	          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
193	          ...(((current as any)[bucket] as any[]) ?? []),
194	          {
195	            exerciseRef,
196	            sectionIndex,
197	            code,
198	            message,
199	            suggestedAction: action,
200	            resolved: false,
201	          },
202	        ],
203	      } as never,
204	      overrideAccess: true,
205	    })
206	  } catch (err) {
207	    logger.error(
208	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef, code, bucket },
209	      `Failed to append ${bucket} entry to LessonDuplications`,
210	    )
211	    // Don't rethrow — streaming failure should not abort the run
212	  }
213	}

296	/**

300	 * which dropped fields like accessType, visibleRenderers, blocks, contentStatus,
301	 * and any custom flags the source lesson had. This caused variation outputs to
302	 * be thinner than what the `none` endpoint produces. We now mirror the
303	 * deep-clone behaviour of the `none` path so both routes produce equivalent
304	 * lessons modulo the title suffix and a forced `draft` status.
305	 */
306	async function createOutputLesson(
307	  payload: Payload,
308	  sourceLessonId: string,
309	  level: string,
310	): Promise<string> {

312	    collection: 'lessons',
313	    id: sourceLessonId,
314	    depth: 0,
315	    overrideAccess: true,

/**
 * Generate a new exercise via the appropriate strategy.
 *
 * K3: ScriptVariationStrategy for purely-algebraic exercises at light level.
 * K4: AiVariationStrategy for all other cases (throws until AI is wired up).
 *
 * @param exercise    Source exercise to vary
 * @param level       Duplication level (none/light/medium/deep)
 * @param _payload    Payload instance (available for future AI strategy use)
 * @returns           StrategyResult with generated blocks and strategy used
 */
export async function runStrategy(
  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
  subject: DuplicationSubject,
  payload: Payload,
): Promise<StrategyResult & { tokensUsed: { inputTokens: number; outputTokens: number } }> {
  const router = new RouterStrategy(payload)
  const result = await router.apply(exercise as unknown as Exercise, level, subject)

  const content = result.exercise.content as unknown as { blocks: ContentBlock[] }
  return {
    exerciseId: exercise.id,
    strategy: result.needsAiFallback ? 'ai' : 'script',
    blocks: content.blocks ?? [],
    tokensUsed: result.tokensUsed ?? { inputTokens: 0, outputTokens: 0 },
  }
}

/**
 * Cap source-exercise block count to match the structural validator's
 * `TOO_MANY_SECTIONS` ceiling (5). Pure: returns a NEW exercise object with
 * a trimmed `content.blocks` array when needed, otherwise the original.
 *
 * Picks 5 representative blocks from the source via `selectSectionsScaled` —
 * the same scaling-random bucketing the lesson-level selector uses. Preserves
 * source order, so the variation reads like a natural shorter version of the
 * original rather than a random shuffle.
 *
 * Rationale: without this, a 10-block source always failed validation
